Martyrs at Merum in Phrygia, under Julian.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Martyrs at Merum in Phrygia, under Julian.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Amachius governor of Phrygia ordered that the temple at Merum, a city of that province, should be opened, and cleared of the filth which had accumulated there by lapse of time: also that the statues it contained should be polished fresh.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This in being put into operation grieved the Christians very much.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now a certain Macedonius and Theodulus and Tatian, unable to endure the indignity thus put upon their religion, and impelled by a fervent zeal for virtue, rushed by night into the temple, and broke the images in pieces.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The governor infuriated at what had been done, would have put to death many in that city who were altogether innocent, when the authors of the deed voluntarily surrendered themselves, choosing rather to die themselves in defense of the truth, than to see others put to death in their stead.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The governor seized and ordered them to expiate the crime they had committed by sacrificing: on their refusal to do this, their judge menaced them with tortures; but they despising his threats, being endowed with great courage, declared their readiness to undergo any sufferings, rather than pollute themselves by sacrificing.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: After subjecting them to all possible tortures he at last laid them on gridirons under which a fire was placed, and thus slew them.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But even in this last extremity they gave the most heroic proofs of fortitude, addressing the ruthless governor thus: ‘If you wish to eat broiled flesh, Amachius, turn us on the other side also, lest we should appear but half cooked to your taste.’

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Thus these martyrs ended their life.
